item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this is too powerful. Assuming anything even remotely like this ever appears in the wild, it would eradicate humanity within a few generations. Even if some GOI planted it on purpose to neutralize the Foundation, had they failed to contain it, it would still have sterilized the planet, including the perpetrators and whatever they were after.

And it's immune to everything! Which means the only place it can reasonably exist is the occasional Author On Board cannon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that the rating is so incredibly low

It's not. ~+60 is a very respectable rating for a year old article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy crap. This is honestly brilliant and it shocks me that the rating is so incredibly low for such a great concept, especially after over a year of being on the site. May the +1 angels descend upon this skip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, another canonical 'designation'. I was trying to convey the fact that I whole-heartedly support things that go against the established format. I grasped the wrong end of the stick during my initial scim-read, and assumed it was some kind of secondary object class specific to that Scip. Apologies for the confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What do you think is misspelled here?

  • "TYRFING" wouldn't work if it was a real word.
  • "exhibits potent mnestic properties" is all spelled correctly
  • "Mnestic" is clearly correct here because it goes on to say that +TYRFING persons have improved recall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mnestic means it helps you remember, instead of making you forget. It's used in the Antimemetics tale series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"TYRFING" exhibits potent mnestic properties

Was the mis-spell intentional?

I havent understood the whole scp yet so im just putting this here incase its not intentional

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last sentence the researcher references SCP-2602 without mentioning that it used to be a library, which seems like should be changed maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just to let you know you've used 'sensor' when you meant 'censor' in several places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been passing it around on chat for awhile, so you probably saw it there.

And yes, it infects then deletes itself and other concepts when spread. It's not limited to a single, other concept though; any and every concept the host related to it is lost. (E.g., if TYRFING was the concept of 'apples', you could think 'apples are like pears' and 'what if apples were square?' Then, when the concept is spread, you would forget about - and permanently become unable to percieve - apples, pears, and squares.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! I really hope this survives, partly because it'll give us another canonical Object Class, something that I massively support.

I don't get what you're saying here. The object class is clearly Keter. TYRFING is not the object class; it's the name of the SCP since the SCP's effect is to replace another concept with itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you post this before, or did I see it in chat? O.o The designation and general concept are familiar, at least.

I'm very glad that test log is there, because without it, I was very shaky on what's going on here. But it sounds like an idea that infects a host, then moves on while deleting not only comprehension of itself, but of one other concept as well. Sound about right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also in reference to storage media.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Media is used in reference to mass communications, where media are newspapers, radio, the internet, and so on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it! I really hope this survives, partly because it'll give us another canonical Object Class, something that I massively support.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is media the plural of medium in any context? Just curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you said 'mediums' instead of media

Mediums and media are both acceptable plural forms of medium, depending on context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The subject showed no compulsion to refer to the fact that SCP-2602 used to be a library; when presented with images taken from SCP-2602’s interior, the subject identified it as a former library without difficulty. Interviews with the subject regarding SCP-2602 are impeded by its anomalous properties, as the subject is unable to hear interviewers when they refer to SCP-2602 as a former library. Further research into cross-utilisation of “TYRFING” with SCP-2602 is pending approval.

Whether the test worked or not, the in-universe writer succumbed to the anomaly. Should have let the D-class take the keyboard for this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. Antimemes are difficult to do well, so there just aren't enough good ones. I really like the concept, it's original and I was able to understand it. I noticed a couple of very minor spelling errors, for example you said 'mediums' instead of media, and you said sensor instead of censor. Original and interesting concept. Excellent SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I'm not sure how I feel about this yet, I feel it may take repeated reads. +1 for conceptual bravery if nothing else.

item.title: This most
item.content:encoded:
Inspired by the idea of a 'memetic antimeme', the result is the infoallergen.
(someone else suggested the term 'infoallergen' and i feel bad for not remembering their name ;.;)
